final_output:
# Comprehensive Plan: Multi-Tenant Document Intelligence SaaS Platform

## Codename: **DocMind**

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Executive Summary](#1-executive-summary)
2. [Architecture Overview](#2-architecture-overview)
3. [Data Model & Multi-Tenancy Design](#3-data-model--multi-tenancy-design)
4. [Detailed System Design](#4-detailed-system-design)
5. [API Design](#5-api-design)
6. [Frontend Architecture](#6-frontend-architecture)
7. [Infrastructure & AWS Architecture](#7-infrastructure--aws-architecture)
8. [Sprint Plan & Timeline](#8-sprint-plan--timeline)
9. [Team Responsibilities](#9-team-responsibilities)
10. [Risk Register & Mitigations](#10-risk-register--mitigations)
11. [Cost Estimation](#11-cost-estimation)
12. [Testing Strategy](#12-testing-strategy)
13. [Security & Compliance](#13-security--compliance)
14. [Monitoring & Observability](#14-monitoring--observability)
15. [Key Technical Decisions & ADRs](#15-key-technical-decisions--adrs)
16. [CI/CD Pipeline](#16-cicd-pipeline)
17. [Quick Start / Local Development Setup](#17-quick-start--local-development-setup)

---

## 1. Executive Summary

DocMind is a multi-tenant SaaS platform enabling organizations to upload documents, automatically extract text, generate AI-powered summaries, and ask natural-language questions answered via Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). The platform serves 100 tenants with strict data isolation, role-based access control, and scales to 1 million total documents.

**Key architectural choices:**
- Shared database with row-level tenant isolation (tenant_id on every table + PostgreSQL Row-Level Security)
- Asynchronous document processing pipeline using Celery with SQS as broker and Redis (ElastiCache) as result backend
- pgvector (≥ 0.5.0) for vector embeddings (avoiding a separate vector DB to reduce operational complexity)
- Hybrid retrieval: pgvector cosine similarity + PostgreSQL full-text search (tsvector) with Reciprocal Rank Fusion
- OpenAI API for embeddings and LLM inference (with abstraction layer for future model swaps)
- S3 for document storage with tenant-prefixed paths and presigned upload URLs
- MVP (weeks 1–8) uses polling for async status; WebSocket/SSE streaming added in Phase 2 (weeks 9–16)

---

## 2. Architecture Overview

### 2.1 High-Level Architecture Diagram

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                          CLIENTS                                     │
│   React SPA (CloudFront + S3 Static Hosting)                         │
└──────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                       │ HTTPS
                       ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    AWS Application Load Balancer                      │
│                    (SSL termination, path routing)                    │
└──────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                       │
                       ▼
              ┌──────────────────┐
              │   API Service     │
              │   (FastAPI)       │
              │   ECS Fargate     │
              │   2-8 tasks       │
              │                   │
              │  [Phase 2: adds   │
              │   /ws + SSE       │
              │   for streaming]  │
              └────────┬─────────┘
                       │
         ┌─────────────┼──────────────────┐
         ▼             ▼                  ▼
┌──────────────┐ ┌─────────────┐  ┌──────────────────┐
│  PostgreSQL   │ │   Redis      │  │   Amazon SQS      │
│  (RDS)        │ │(ElastiCache) │  │   (Celery Broker)  │
│  + pgvector   │ │(Result Bknd) │  │                    │
│    ≥ 0.5.0    │ │ + caching    │  │                    │
│  + tsvector   │ │ + rate limit │  │                    │
└──────────────┘ └─────────────┘  └────────┬───────────┘
                                           │
                                           ▼
                                  ┌──────────────────┐
                                  │  Celery Workers    │
                                  │  ECS Fargate       │
                                  │  2-10 tasks        │
                                  │                    │
                                  │  ┌──────────────┐  │
                                  │  │ Text Extract  │  │
                                  │  │ Embedding Gen │  │
                                  │  │ Summary Gen   │  │
                                  │  └──────────────┘  │
                                  └────────┬───────────┘
                                           │
                             ┌─────────────┼──────────────┐
                             ▼             ▼              ▼
                       ┌──────────┐ ┌───────────┐ ┌────────────┐
                       │   S3      │ │  OpenAI   │ │ PostgreSQL │
                       │ (docs)    │ │  API      │ │ (vectors)  │
                       └──────────┘ └───────────┘ └────────────┘
```

### 2.2 Request Flow: Document Upload → Q&A

```
Upload Flow:
  User → API → Validate (auth + RBAC: admin/editor) → Validate file type + size
       → Generate presigned S3 URL (15-min expiry, content-type enforced, 50MB max)
       → Client uploads directly to S3 via presigned URL
       → Client calls POST /documents/confirm with S3 key
       → Insert DB record (status: pending) → Enqueue SQS message
       → Return 202 Accepted with document_id

Processing Flow (async):
  Worker picks SQS message → Download from S3
       → Extract text (pdfplumber for PDF / python-docx for DOCX)
       → Chunk text (recursive splitter, respecting paragraph/heading boundaries,
         512 tokens, 50 token overlap)
       → Generate tsvector for each chunk (full-text search)
       → Generate embeddings (OpenAI text-embedding-3-small, 1536 dims)
       → Store chunks + embeddings + tsvector in PostgreSQL
       → Generate summary (OpenAI GPT-4o-mini)
       → Update DB record (status: ready)
       → Store result in Redis for polling

Status Check (MVP - Polling):
  Client polls GET /documents/{id} every 3s → Returns current status
  [Phase 2]: SSE pushes status update to connected client

Q&A Flow:
  User asks question → API → Check tenant token budget (atomic Redis check)
       → Generate query embedding (text-embedding-3-small)
       → Hybrid retrieval:
           1. Vector similarity search (pgvector, cosine distance, top-20)
           2. Full-text search (tsvector, ts_rank, top-20)
           3. Reciprocal Rank Fusion to merge results → top-5
       → Filter by tenant_id + document_scope (enforced by RLS)
       → Construct prompt with context + citation instructions
       → Call LLM (GPT-4o-mini) → Return response with source citations
       → [Phase 2]: Stream response via SSE
       → Log token usage: atomic Redis increment, async sync to PostgreSQL
```

---

## 3. Data Model & Multi-Tenancy Design

### 3.1 Multi-Tenancy Strategy

**Approach: Shared database, shared schema, row-level isolation**

**Rationale:**
- 100 tenants is manageable in a single database
- Minimizes operational overhead (single RDS instance)
- PostgreSQL Row-Level Security (RLS) provides defense-in-depth
- Application-level tenant filtering as primary mechanism
- RLS as a safety net preventing cross-tenant data leaks

```sql
-- Enable RLS on all tenant-scoped tables
-- The app sets this at the start of every DB session/transaction:
-- SET LOCAL app.current_tenant_id = '<tenant-uuid>';
ALTER TABLE documents ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY tenant_isolation ON documents
    USING (tenant_id = current_setting('app.current_tenant_id')::UUID);
```

### 3.2 Database Schema

```sql
-- ============================================
-- TENANT & USER MANAGEMENT
-- ============================================

CREATE TABLE tenants (
    id                UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    name              VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    slug              VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    plan              VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'standard',
    settings          JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
    max_documents     INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 10000,
    max_users         INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 50,
    monthly_token_budget   BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 10000000,
    tokens_used_this_month BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    budget_reset_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT date_trunc('month', NOW()) + INTERVAL '1 month',
    max_queries_per_hour   INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 100,
    is_active         BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
    created_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE TABLE users (
    id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    tenant_id       UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),
    email           VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    password_hash   VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    full_name       VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    role            VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL CHECK (role IN ('admin', 'editor', 'viewer')),
    is_active       BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
    last_login_at   TIMESTAMPTZ,
    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    UNIQUE(tenant_id, email)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_users_tenant_id ON users(tenant_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_users_email ON users(email);

-- ============================================
-- DOCUMENT MANAGEMENT
-- ============================================

CREATE TABLE documents (
    id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    tenant_id       UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),
    uploaded_by     UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    filename        VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
    file_type       VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL CHECK (file_type IN ('pdf', 'docx')),
    file_size_bytes BIGINT NOT NULL,
    s3_key          VARCHAR(1000) NOT NULL,
    status          VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'
                    CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'processing', 'ready', 'failed')),
    error_message   TEXT,
    summary         TEXT,
    page_count      INTEGER,
    word_count      INTEGER,
    metadata        JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_documents_tenant_id ON documents(tenant_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_documents_tenant_status ON documents(tenant_id, status);
CREATE INDEX idx_documents_uploaded_by ON documents(uploaded_by);

-- ============================================
-- TEXT CHUNKS & VECTOR EMBEDDINGS
-- ============================================

CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;

CREATE TABLE document_chunks (
    id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    document_id     UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    tenant_id       UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),
    chunk_index     INTEGER NOT NULL,
    content         TEXT NOT NULL,
    token_count     INTEGER NOT NULL,
    page_number     INTEGER,
    heading_context VARCHAR(500),  -- nearest heading for retrieval context
    embedding       vector(1536),
    content_tsv     tsvector GENERATED ALWAYS AS (to_tsvector('english', content)) STORED,
    metadata        JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_chunks_document_id ON document_chunks(document_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_chunks_tenant_id ON document_chunks(tenant_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_chunks_content_tsv ON document_chunks USING GIN(content_tsv);

-- IVFFlat index: lists=1000 suitable for MVP scale (~5M chunks).
-- At 50M+ chunks, evaluate HNSW (pgvector ≥ 0.5.0) which doesn't require
-- reindexing and has better recall. See ADR-005 for migration plan.
CREATE INDEX idx_chunks_embedding ON document_chunks
    USING ivfflat (embedding vector_cosine_ops) WITH (lists = 1000);

-- ============================================
-- CONVERSATIONS & Q&A
-- ============================================

CREATE TABLE conversations (
    id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    tenant_id       UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),
    user_id         UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    title           VARCHAR(500),
    document_scope  UUID[] NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_conversations_tenant_user ON conversations(tenant_id, user_id);

CREATE TABLE messages (
    id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    conversation_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES conversations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    tenant_id       UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),
    role            VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL CHECK (role IN ('user', 'assistant')),
    content         TEXT NOT NULL,
    source_chunks   UUID[] DEFAULT '{}',
    token_usage     JSONB,
    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_messages_conversation ON messages(conversation_id, created_at);

-- ============================================
-- TOKEN USAGE TRACKING
-- ============================================

CREATE TABLE token_usage_log (
    id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    tenant_id       UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),
    user_id         UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    operation       VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    model           VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    prompt_tokens   INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    completion_tokens INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    total_tokens    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_token_usage_tenant_created ON token_usage_log(tenant_id, created_at DESC);

-- ============================================
-- AUDIT LOG
-- ============================================

CREATE TABLE audit_logs (
    id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    tenant_id       UUID NOT NULL,
    user_id         UUID,
    action          VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    resource_type   VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    resource_id     UUID,
    details         JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
    ip_address      INET,
    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_audit_tenant_created ON audit_logs(tenant_id, created_at DESC);

-- ============================================
-- ROW-LEVEL SECURITY (all tenant-scoped tables)
-- ============================================

ALTER TABLE documents ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE document_chunks ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE conversations ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE messages ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE users ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE token_usage_log ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE audit_logs ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY tenant_isolation_documents ON documents
    FOR ALL USING (tenant_id = current_setting('app.current_tenant_id')::UUID);
